title: Phaseoloides pendulum
taxonomy: Phaseoloides pendulum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae, native to tropical Africa. It is a climbing vine with a twining habit, often found in woodland areas and along streams. The classification of Phaseoloides pendulum is further categorized under the subfamily Faboideae and the tribe Phaseoleae. The species name pendulum refers to the plant's climbing habit, where it uses its tendrils to swing and support itself on nearby structures. This classification helps identify Phaseoloides pendulum as a unique and specialized species within the larger pea family.
